h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.statedInsightEvidence: So the count has to be two across times two down, not just “double the side, double the area.”
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner was moving from the misbelief that doubling the side doubles the area toward the correct calculation, with each turn clarifying the reasoning.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Start with the claim, not the answer. Use two tiles first. Cover the doubled square, no gaps, no overhang, while the outside edges stay two old lengths across and two old lengths down. Stop where the grid refuses.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor shifts from grid-based visual reasoning to a hands-on tile-covering task, introducing a new representation that directly confronts the learner's misconception.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor adaptation to a revised framing.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No pivot learner turn exists, so no later tutor adaptation to a revised framing.
